case.label: The Hijacking
case.solution: Cipher — in reality a woman with a direct, still-hidden connection to Harrow's dead — built a backdoor into Crestline's fleet telematics months ago specifically to seize control of car #114 the night Elias Vance booked it, using escalating criminal tasks and a live explosive charge wired into the trunk as leverage to force Marcus and Elias to the ruins of Harrow Warehouse and make them answer, in real time, for the night the east wing came down.
objective_facts: Cipher's countdown reaches its final phase the moment the sedan comes to rest inside Harrow's fenced perimeter.
Cipher has followed the sedan's arrival on foot, staying masked and at a deliberate distance from the vehicle's headlights.
Cipher has not yet detonated the device despite having every technical means to do so at will.

knows: Cipher is physically present at Harrow tonight, not just a voice on a speaker — though she keeps herself masked and in shadow.
hides: That some part of him recognizes something in how she moves or speaks up close — a familiarity he can't yet place and isn't ready to say aloud.

knows: The person behind tonight's voice is standing somewhere near the wreck, close enough to see but not yet close enough to identify.
hides: That he's more frightened of who this person might turn out to be than of the bomb itself.
believes_falsely: That once he sees her face, he'll recognize a business rival or disgruntled former employee — not someone with no professional connection to him at all.

knows: Both men are finally in the one place she's spent months engineering this night to bring them to.
hides: Her face and her real name — she keeps herself masked even now, unwilling to let the reveal come before the truth does.

case.label: The Harrow Reckoning
case.solution: Ten years ago, foreman Marcus Kane discovered that Vance Development's riverside redevelopment used undersized support beams to hit a construction deadline; he reported it up the chain, but VP Elias Vance overruled the warning to protect the project's schedule and investors. The east wing collapsed days later, killing several workers including apprentice electrician Danny Su. Vance Development quietly settled with Marcus for his silence rather than let his report become public, and Marcus took the payout and disappeared into chauffeuring rather than ever coming forward. In the weeks after the settlement, wracked with guilt, Marcus anonymously mailed Wei Su a letter and a portion of his own settlement money as an apology; believing it was hush money sent by Vance Development itself, Wei Su burned it unopened by the return address, never knowing who it was truly from.
objective_facts: Marcus Kane filed an internal safety report three days before the Harrow Warehouse collapse, flagging undersized support beams in the east wing.

Elias Vance, then Vance Development's VP of Operations, personally signed off on proceeding with construction despite the report — and had a second engineer's follow-up warning quietly dismissed in the same week.
Danny Su, a 24-year-old apprentice electrician and Wei Su's son, died in the east wing collapse.

In the weeks after his settlement, Marcus anonymously mailed Wei Su a letter and a portion of his own money as an apology; Wei Su, believing it was hush money from Vance Development, burned it unopened by the return address.

knows: He mailed Wei Su an anonymous apology and money years ago, and never learned whether it was received.
hides: Nothing further to hide from Elias tonight — but he still hasn't found the words to say any of this directly to Wei Su's face.
believes_falsely: That his own silence, not Elias's decision to bury a second warning too, was the single deciding factor in Danny's death.

knows: He dismissed not just Marcus's report but a second engineer's follow-up warning in that same week — a fact he has never admitted to anyone until Cipher forces it out of him tonight.
hides: Nothing left to hide about the collapse itself — but he still hasn't decided whether to tell Marcus he now believes Marcus tried, in his own way, to make amends.

knows: An anonymous letter and money arrived at his door weeks after Danny's death, which he burned without reading, believing it was Vance Development trying to buy his silence.
hides: That some nights he's regretted burning it before he even understood what it might have been.
believes_falsely: Until tonight, that the letter could only have come from Vance Development itself — never once suspecting it might have been from the driver of a construction crew he'd never met by name.

episodes.theme: The season's reckoning lands — the hijacking ends and Harrow's true account is settled between the three people who share it, while what Marcus, Elias, and Delia do with each other once the night is over remains an open question (The Hijacking resolves; The Harrow Reckoning resolves; aftermath of The Chase).
episodes.intro_note: The trunk lock finally released on Cipher's command, and the screen bolted inside it lit up with a face neither man in the sedan had ever seen — a woman standing somewhere close, watching them through the car's own cameras. A countdown began ticking in the corner of that screen. Outside the fence, in the grey light before dawn, an old man in a watchman's coat was already walking toward the sound of an idling engine he recognized without knowing why.
sections: Dawn Breaks Over Harrow — Wei Su crosses the frozen ground toward a sedan that has no business being here (Harrow Reckoning)
The Face in the Trunk — Marcus and Elias see Cipher for the first time, and neither man says what he recognizes (Hijacking)
The Old Watchman's Recognition — Wei Su reaches the fence and hears a voice he has waited ten years to hear again (Harrow Reckoning)
What the Report Really Said — Cipher speaks directly to Marcus about the certification he signed (Harrow Reckoning; Hijacking)
Trace Complete — Chen's team locates the intrusion's final relay and moves (Chase)
Breaking Protocol — Delia leaves her post against direct orders to reach the site (Chase)
The Payoff — Elias forces the truth out of Marcus in front of the one man both of them have been afraid of (Harrow Reckoning)
Father and Daughter — Wei Su speaks to Mei through the open window of a car she has spent all night controlling (Harrow Reckoning; Hijacking)
The Countdown — the timer on the trunk device reaches its final minute (Hijacking)
Arrival — Chen and Delia reach the fence line as the standoff peaks (Chase)
Disengage — Mei chooses exposure over detonation (Hijacking resolves)
Doors Unlocked — Marcus and Elias step out of the sedan for the first time since the hijacking began (Hijacking resolves)
The Long Fare's Last Mile — a quiet morning at Marcus's apartment leaves the season's real question unanswered (aftermath)

case.label: The Hijacking
case.solution: Cipher is Mei Su, daughter of Wei Su and sister of Danny Su, a young electrician killed in the Harrow collapse. Trained as a security engineer, she was later contracted to service Crestline Chauffeur Service's fleet software and used that access to plant the backdoor that let her seize the sedan's systems tonight. She engineered the entire hijacking — including a repurposed demolition charge hidden in the trunk, salvaged from decommissioned Vance Development equipment — to trap Marcus and Elias together long enough to force a confession, intending to detonate the charge against Harrow's condemned east wing to destroy the buried vault of falsified reports and payoff records hidden there, not to kill either man, though she let both believe otherwise all night. Confronted by her father at the last minute, she disengages the detonator and instead releases the vault's contents — the unaltered inspection reports and settlement records — directly to Detective Chen's bureau and the press.
objective_facts: The sedan's onboard systems were compromised through a backdoor installed months earlier while Mei Su was contracted to service Crestline's fleet software.
The trunk contained a rigged demolition charge repurposed from decommissioned Vance Development equipment, alongside a data drive holding the unaltered original Harrow inspection reports.
Mei Su disabled the charge's detonator herself at the fence line before dawn.
Once Mei surfaces voluntarily, Detective Chen's team confirms the intrusion's origin and terminates the remaining access to the sedan's systems.

knows: He has been trapped in a hijacked vehicle all night with a stranger's voice controlling everything.
He now sees the hacker's face for the first time and does not recognize her — until she names the report he signed.
hides: He briefly considers lying about the report even now, out of a decade-old habit of protecting himself first.
believes_falsely: Until this episode, he assumed the voice was some rival of Elias's, with no connection to the collapse itself.

knows: He is being forced to confront a version of himself he paid heavily, years ago, to bury.
He recognizes the demolition charge's origin as Vance Development salvage the moment he sees it on screen.
hides: He still hopes, even now, that exposure can be managed the way the original collapse was — with money and lawyers.
believes_falsely: He believed the settlements had permanently closed every file connected to Harrow; he has no idea a vault of originals survived.

knows: Every falsified detail of the Harrow certification, and exactly what leverage each man in the sedan fears losing most.
That her father is standing at the fence and will hear whatever she says next.
hides: Until her father speaks, she conceals that the charge was never meant to hurt anyone — she needed both men to believe their lives were at stake.
believes_falsely: She believed destroying the vault physically was the only way anyone would ever believe her; she has not considered that exposure without violence could work just as well until her father asks her to try.

knows: The intrusion's final relay traces to a location near Harrow Warehouse.
The sedan's systems are still live and under external control as his team approaches.
believes_falsely: He expects, right up until arrival, that he is chasing a criminal extortion ring, not a single grieving engineer.

knows: Marcus's GPS ping has led directly to the one ruin he has never once mentioned to her in ten years of friendship.
She is riding into whatever this is against her supervisor's direct order.
believes_falsely: She still half-believes, until she sees his face at the fence, that Marcus is purely a victim tonight and nothing more.

knows: The voice controlling the sedan belongs to his daughter, alive, the moment he hears it through the open window.
He recognizes the demolition charge for what it represents — the vault he always suspected was sealed inside the collapsed wing.
believes_falsely: He believed Mei had simply vanished out of grief; he never imagined she had spent a decade building the exact skills needed to do this.

case.label: The Harrow Reckoning
case.solution: Ten years ago, Elias Vance ordered the retrofit of Harrow Warehouse's east wing completed two weeks ahead of its engineered schedule to meet a redevelopment deadline. Marcus Kane, then site foreman, was pressured by Elias's site manager into signing a false safety certification despite being shown photographs of structural cracking, in exchange for a payoff he used to cover his sister's medical bills. Wei Su, the night watchman, had filed written warnings about the cracking three weeks before the collapse; those warnings were buried before reaching city inspectors. The east wing came down during a night shift, killing three workers, including Wei Su's son Danny, an apprentice electrician. Vance Development quietly settled with the families under non-disclosure agreements within six months, and the official inquiry blamed worker error, implicitly discrediting Wei Su for not escalating further. Marcus left construction entirely and became a chauffeur to disappear; Wei Su took the settlement to survive and has kept vigil at the ruin ever since, never telling his surviving daughter Mei the details of what he signed away in silence.
objective_facts: Vance Development ordered the Harrow east wing retrofit completed two weeks ahead of its original engineering timeline.
Marcus Kane signed the final safety certification after being shown photographs of structural cracking in the wing.
Wei Su's written warnings, filed three weeks before the collapse, never reached city inspectors.
Three workers died in the collapse, including Danny Su, an apprentice electrician and Wei Su's son.
Vance Development paid settlements to victims' families under non-disclosure agreements within six months of the collapse.

knows: He signed the false certification for money he needed for his sister's care, and has carried that decision every day since.
He now says this aloud, in full, for the first time — to Elias, to Mei, and to Wei Su.
believes_falsely: He long believed his silence spared Wei Su's family nothing worse than they'd already suffered; he has never considered that his signature is the one fact Wei Su has spent ten years wanting to hear.

knows: He ordered the schedule that made the collapse possible, and approved the settlements that kept it from ever becoming public.
He now admits, in front of Wei Su, that the inquiry's finding of worker error was a finding his own lawyers shaped.
believes_falsely: He has told himself for ten years that the settlements were an act of responsibility rather than concealment; tonight is the first time he says the word 'cover-up' about his own decision.

knows: His warnings were filed and buried, and his son died three weeks later in the exact spot he'd flagged.
The settlement he accepted bought his family's survival and his silence in the same signature.
hides: He has never told Mei precisely how much money he took, or that he suspected Marcus's certification specifically, out of shame.
believes_falsely: He believed he was the only one who still carried the weight of that night; hearing Marcus's and Elias's admissions in the same hour is the first time he learns how much they, too, have carried.

knows: Every detail of the falsified certification and the buried warnings, assembled over years from records her father never knew she'd found.
That her brother's death was preventable by weeks, not chance.
She learns, for the first time tonight, that Marcus was the foreman who signed off on Harrow — the thing he never once told her in ten years of friendship.
believes_falsely: She had always assumed Marcus's silence about his past was about grief alone; she now has to reconcile the friend she knows with the choice he made.

case.label: The Chase
case.solution: Detective Chen's bureau traces the hijacking signal's final relay to a decommissioned Vance Development server still connected to Crestline's fleet network, leading his team to Harrow Warehouse. Delia, informed by Chen, abandons her dispatch post against her supervisor's direct order to ride with his team, arriving at the fence line just as Mei disables the detonator — close enough to see Marcus step out of the sedan, too late to have changed anything that happened inside it.
objective_facts: Chen's bureau traces the intrusion's final relay to Harrow Warehouse roughly twenty minutes before the sedan itself arrives there.
Delia leaves her dispatch post against direct orders to accompany Chen's team to the site.
Chen's team reaches the fence line at Harrow Warehouse after Mei Su has already disabled the detonator.

knows: The relay's physical origin and the fastest route to it.
That his team is arriving after the most dangerous moment, not before it.
believes_falsely: He expects to be the one who stops the threat; he arrives to find it has already been stopped by the person who created it.

knows: She broke protocol to be here, and would do it again.
Marcus is alive and walking, and that is the only fact she came for.
Delia came for him, against every rule of her job.
believes_falsely: He assumed, all night, that no one outside the sedan would ever know or care what happened to him specifically — he is unprepared for how much Delia clearly does.
